commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Slack block-action rich-text preview truncation to use `truncateSlackText` and adds boundary tests for emoji surrogate pairs.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +90. Total +91 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main and v2026.6.10 still use raw UTF-16 slicing in the rich-text preview path, and the PR body's terminal demo shows the boundary case producing a lone surrogate before the fix.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because the PR already implements the narrow helper-based fix; remaining work is maintainer review and merge handling.

target.title: fix(slack): truncate rich text preview on a UTF-16 boundary
target.body:
**Summary:** The Slack block-action rich-text preview was truncated on raw UTF-16, tearing an emoji at the boundary into a lone surrogate. This truncates on a code-point boundary.

## What Problem This Solves

Slack rich text interaction previews were truncated with raw UTF-16 slicing. When a `rich_text_input` value placed an emoji on the preview boundary, the old slice could keep only the high surrogate half of the emoji before appending the ellipsis.

Trigger input proven here: 118 ASCII characters followed by 😀 and trailing text. The previous `.slice(0, 119) + "…"` behavior produced a preview ending in `\uD83D…`, leaving a lone surrogate in the Slack interaction payload.

## Fix

The Slack block action rich text preview path now reuses the shared `truncateUtf16Safe` helper from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-utility-runtime` instead of raw slicing.

The preview still enforces the existing 120 UTF-16 code unit cap, reserves one code unit for the ellipsis, and only truncates when the joined rich text exceeds that cap. The existing Slack confirmation path continues escaping the selected label with `escapeSlackMrkdwn` before sending it back to Slack.

## Evidence

Command run from the isolated PR worktree:

```bash
node --import tsx demo.mts
```

The demo imports the real fixed `summarizeAction` function from `extensions/slack/src/monitor/events/interactions.block-actions.ts`, builds a Slack `rich_text_input` payload with an emoji on the truncation boundary, and compares the old raw-slice behavior with the fixed rich text preview path.

```text
input.length= 124
input.cutBoundary= 0061 d83d de00 0074
BEFORE old .slice(0, 119)+ellipsis= "aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa…[118 ×'a' elided]\ud83d…"
BEFORE tail code units= 0061 d83d 2026
BEFORE lone-surrogate= true
AFTER summarizeAction.richTextPreview= "aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa…[118 ×'a' elided]…"
AFTER tail code units= 0061 0061 2026
AFTER lone-surrogate= false
```

The fixed output is well-formed UTF-16: the boundary emoji is dropped whole when it would straddle the preview limit, and the lone-surrogate detector reports `false`.

## Tests

Targeted Vitest coverage was run in the isolated PR worktree:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/events/interactions.test.ts extensions/slack/src/truncate.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  54 passed (54)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 54.64s
```

Coverage includes the Slack rich text interaction payload path that drops a boundary emoji whole, verifies `richTextPreview.length <= 120`, checks no lone surrogate remains, and confirms URI encoding does not throw. It also covers the shared Slack truncation helper behavior for plain BMP text, emojis that fit before the cut, and trimmed inputs that do not need truncation.
